meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on factor notation and cash flow timing
meta.description: Uniform series, gradients, and factor relationships for time value calculations

patterns.name: Annuity Present Worth
patterns.recognition_signal: Equal payments converted to present lump sum
patterns.logic: Convert uniform series to equivalent present amount

efficiency_trick.name: P Location
steps: P is always ONE PERIOD BEFORE first A

pitfalls.name: Timing error
pitfalls.description: Placing P at same time as first A

patterns.name: Loan Payment Calculation
patterns.recognition_signal: Present amount converted to equal payments
patterns.logic: Capital recovery - paying back loan in uniform installments

patterns.name: Linear Gradient Series
patterns.recognition_signal: Cash flows changing by constant amount

pitfalls.name: First G location
pitfalls.description: First gradient amount at period 2, not 1

decision_tree.question: What is the cash flow pattern?
What direction?
decision_tree.yes: Present to future (F/P), future to present (P/F), uniform to present (P/A)

professor_tips: Memorize the six basic factor formulas
Understand cash flow timing for each factor
Practice drawing the cash flow diagrams

confidence_checklist: I know all six factor formulas
I understand cash flow timing for each factor
I can handle annuity due vs ordinary annuity
I can decompose complex cash flows
